summary_one_line: A well-formed scheme://host:port/path URL whose connection is reusable (conn->bits.close FALSE), reaching the connection-keep-intact else arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured request input that parses into a valid scheme://host:port/path URL (e.g. the bytes 70 6f 25 ... 3a 2f 2f '://' host '12' ':9001/850' and a 'GHTTP/27.0.' line with a long '////' path) so the resulting connection is cleanly completed and NOT marked for close, driving the multi_done branch '|| conn->bits.close' to FALSE and taking the connection-reuse else arm at line 718.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry a coherent URL skeleton: offset 0x06-0x0f spells 'po%.://12%/0((.1:9001/850\0' and offset 0x24 onward is 'GHTTP/27.0.' followed by a long '/' run, so the request reaches multi_done with conn->bits.close FALSE (L711 W=T:0/F:17, the false side) and proceeds into the else reuse block (lines 718-742 marked [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35 bytes) and byte-noise dominated by '%','+','A' fills (e.g. seed 2 '%`%a>f..c%+]%A.AAAA@AAZpermit'), so they never build a reusable connection; they always take the true disconnect arm (L711 L=T:20/F:0) executing connclose/Curl_disconnect at lines 713-716 marked [L].
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which reads intercepted CMP operands (URL-scheme, host, port, HTTP-version literal comparisons in curl's parser) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the structured URL bytes that naive/value_profile cannot assemble by blind havoc; the winning cmplog seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows confirming an I2S contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs at this branch differ by the I2S technique and both favor the I2S-equipped fuzzer.
The second decisive pair is cmplog beating naive on the same I2S axis.
The blocked side is the false branch of the conn->bits.close test in multi_done.
The winner takes the else connection-reuse arm of the multi_done close test.
The connection-reuse else block (lines 718-742) is executed only by winner-resolving seeds.
The disconnect/connclose true arm (lines 713-716) is executed only by loser-blocking seeds.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false side and the loser the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 711  T=0 F=17  T=20 F=0  ) || conn->bits.close  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry a structured URL skeleton with scheme/host/port and an HTTP-version literal.
The winner seed contains a GHTTP version literal and a long slash-path run.
Loser-blocking seeds are short noise-filled inputs lacking a coherent URL structure.
cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of naive's stack.
I2SRandReplace substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching byte offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile does not have I2SRandReplace, explaining why vpc beats it on the I2S axis.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 298 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 32d10b449c4075ee` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 23 and 24 of the 25-level ancestor chain; the tool's trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line confirmed them as unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds characteristic of cmplog/vpc.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive/value_profile resolve the conn->bits.close==FALSE reuse arm at the same rate as cmplog/vpc once the URL-parsing CMP literals are removed, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S operand substitution into the structured URL.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP comparison whose operand I2S substitutes to flip conn->bits.close to FALSE is inferred from the URL-shaped winner bytes and curl's parser, not directly observed at the comparison site.
